meta.purpose: Reusable content brief templates for IoTEdges SEO page planning before Markdown content generation.

global_rules.language: English first for export SEO; Chinese planning notes may be kept in internal docs.
claim_policy: Do not claim unsupported certifications, protocol support, environmental ratings, security features, or compliance.
Use conservative wording for sample or planned case studies until real data is available.
Separate factual protocol explanations from product capability claims.
required_before_drafting: Confirm product capability and unsupported candidate fields.
Confirm target route and canonical slug.
Confirm factual source cards for technical claims.
common_conversion_elements: links to relevant products and solutions

templates.name: Product Page Brief
required_sections: Hero: product category, primary value proposition, and inquiry CTA
faq_prompts: What devices can this product connect to?
Which protocols are supported?
Can it send data to an MQTT broker or cloud platform?
Is it suitable for remote sites?
How do I choose IO and connectivity options?
quality_checks: No unsupported protocol or certification claims.
At least 5 internal links are included.
Product page links to at least 3 solution pages.
FAQ answers are concise and factual.
CTA is commercial but not pushy.

templates.name: Protocol Product Page Brief
faq_prompts: What is the difference between this gateway and a generic IoT gateway?
Can it convert legacy industrial devices to MQTT or Ethernet?
How many devices can be connected?
What should be checked before deployment?
Which applications commonly use this gateway?
quality_checks: Protocol explanation matches source cards.
Architecture is technically plausible.
Product claims are capability-validated.
Links to both product pillar and protocol knowledge page.
Includes at least 2 application examples.

templates.name: Solution Page Brief
required_sections: Hero: solution outcome and target application
faq_prompts: What data can be monitored in this application?
Which IoTEdges products are typically used?
Which protocols are common?
Can this work at remote sites?
What information is needed to design a solution?
quality_checks: Links to at least 2 product pages.
Links to at least 2 knowledge base pages.
Links to at least 1 case study or planned case.
No industry compliance claims without proof.
Includes practical implementation checklist.

templates.name: Knowledge Base Brief
required_sections: Where it is used in industrial IoT
faq_prompts: What is this technology?
How is it used in industrial monitoring?
What should engineers check before deployment?
Which IoTEdges products use it?
How does it compare with related technologies?
quality_checks: Every technical claim is source-card backed.
No generic textbook filler.
Includes product and solution links naturally.
Explains practical deployment implications.
Avoids unsupported product claims.

templates.name: Comparison Article Brief
faq_prompts: Which option is better for remote monitoring?
Which option is better for factory systems?
Can both be used together?
What gateway is needed?
What should be checked before choosing?
quality_checks: Comparison is balanced and not artificially absolute.
Includes a clear decision table.
Links to both compared knowledge topics.
Links to relevant product pages.
Avoids unsupported performance claims.

templates.name: How-to Article Brief
faq_prompts: What hardware is required?
Which protocol is used?
What data should be mapped?
How do I test the connection?
What are common mistakes?
quality_checks: Steps are actionable and ordered.
No invented UI settings unless product UI is confirmed.
Examples are generic or source-backed.
Links to product and knowledge pages.
Includes testing checklist.

templates.name: Troubleshooting Article Brief
faq_prompts: What is the most common cause?
How do I isolate wiring from configuration problems?
What settings should be checked first?
When should the gateway be replaced or reconfigured?
What information should be sent to support?
quality_checks: Troubleshooting order goes from simple to advanced.
Does not blame user or vendor.
No unsafe electrical advice.
Includes support information checklist.
Links to relevant protocol guide.

templates.name: Buyer Guide Brief
required_sections: What the buyer is trying to solve
faq_prompts: What specs matter most?
How do I choose the right protocol?
How do I choose wired vs wireless connectivity?
What information should I provide for quotation?
Which IoTEdges product family fits this project?
quality_checks: Commercial but useful, not just sales copy.
Includes decision table.
Links to product pages and solution pages.
Highlights capability validation requirements.
CTA asks for project details.

templates.name: Case Study Brief
faq_prompts: What devices were connected?
Which protocols were used?
What data was monitored?
What result was achieved?
Can this architecture be adapted to similar sites?
quality_checks: No fake customer name.
Metrics are real, anonymized, or clearly conservative.
No unverifiable performance claims.
Links to products, solutions, and knowledge pages.
Case can support at least 2 commercial pages.
